title: Getting an API Key
content: To get an API key, log into your dashboard at dashboard.example.com, navigate to Settings > API Keys, and click 'Generate New Key'. You can create multiple keys for different environments. Keep your API keys secure and never share them publicly. If you suspect a key has been compromised, revoke it immediately and generate a new one.

title: API Authentication Methods
content: Our API supports two authentication methods: API Key authentication (via X-API-Key header) and OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens. API Keys are recommended for server-to-server communication, while OAuth is better for user-facing applications. All API requests must include authentication. Failed authentication returns a 401 Unauthorized response.

title: Rate Limits
content: API rate limits vary by plan: Free tier allows 100 requests/minute, Pro allows 1000 requests/minute, and Enterprise has custom limits. Rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset) are included in all responses. If you exceed the limit, you'll receive a 429 Too Many Requests error. Implement exponential backoff when rate limited.

title: Resetting Your Password
content: To reset your password, go to the login page and click 'Forgot Password'. Enter your email address and we'll send you a password reset link. The link expires after 24 hours. If you don't receive the email, check your spam folder. For security reasons, passwords must be at least 12 characters with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and special characters.

title: Understanding Billing and Invoices
content: Billing occurs monthly on your subscription anniversary date. Invoices are automatically emailed to your billing contact. You can view and download invoices from Settings > Billing. We accept credit cards, bank transfers, and PayPal. For Enterprise customers, we also support annual invoicing with NET-30 payment terms. Contact billing@company.com for custom payment arrangements.

title: Refund Policy
content: We offer a 30-day money-back guarantee for new subscriptions. If you're not satisfied with our service, contact support within 30 days of your initial purchase for a full refund. Refunds for partial months are not available for mid-cycle cancellations. Enterprise contracts have separate refund terms outlined in your agreement. Refund requests are processed within 5-7 business days.

title: Troubleshooting API Errors
content: Common API errors and solutions: 400 Bad Request - Check your request format and required fields. 401 Unauthorized - Verify your API key is correct and not expired. 403 Forbidden - Your account may lack required permissions. 404 Not Found - The resource doesn't exist or you don't have access. 429 Too Many Requests - Implement rate limiting. 500 Server Error - Retry with exponential backoff. For persistent issues, contact support with the error details and request ID.

title: Webhook Configuration
content: Webhooks allow you to receive real-time notifications when events occur. Configure webhooks in Settings > Webhooks. Each webhook requires a URL endpoint that accepts POST requests with a JSON payload. We sign all webhook requests with HMAC-SHA256 using your webhook secret. Verify the signature before processing. Webhooks retry up to 5 times with exponential backoff on failure.

title: Data Export and Portability
content: You can export your data at any time from Settings > Data Export. Choose from CSV, JSON, or XML formats. Exports include all your records, metadata, and audit logs. Large exports are generated asynchronously and emailed when ready. GDPR users have the right to data portability and can request deletion of their data. Contact privacy@company.com for data-related requests.

title: Account Security Best Practices
content: Protect your account with these security practices: 1) Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) in Settings > Security. 2) Use strong, unique passwords. 3) Regularly review active sessions and revoke unfamiliar ones. 4) Rotate API keys periodically. 5) Use environment-specific keys for development and production. 6) Monitor audit logs for suspicious activity. 7) Restrict team member permissions to minimum required access.

title: SDK and Code Examples
content: Official SDKs are available for Python, JavaScript/Node.js, Ruby, Go, and PHP. Install via package manager (pip, npm, gem, etc.). SDKs handle authentication, rate limiting, and error handling automatically. Check our GitHub repository (github.com/company/sdk) for installation instructions and examples. Community SDKs exist for other languages but are not officially supported.

title: Service Status and Uptime
content: Monitor our service status at status.example.com. We maintain 99.9% uptime SLA for Pro and Enterprise plans. Scheduled maintenance is announced 48 hours in advance via email and status page. Incident updates are posted in real-time on the status page. Historical uptime data and incident reports are available for transparency. For critical outages, contact support@company.com or our emergency hotline.
